High water conditions; channel control. Channel is clear and ice free.  ADCP mmnt using a cablefox on from the cableway. Uncertainty: 13.6%: can be attributed to the loss of BT over the cross-section due to depth and channel (rocks) interferance. The r/b edge was extrapolated due to shallow depth and the inability of the ADCP to measure close to the edge.  2.00 metres below gauge QREV 3.43 used, moving bed had some issues so GGA was applied instead, same result as WR2 output. This improved the uncertainty to 6.9%. Sources:eHSN v1.2.4

Measurement conducted on cableway with CableFox. Was necessary to use truck winch to hoist CableFox to platform level. One CC conducted and used. Typical Heading Error: < 0.826°. Vector Addition Of Single And Double Cycle Errors: 1.103°. MBT Loop 000 passes criteria and indicates no moving bed. BT used as reference. Some missing/bad data occurs during traverse of shallow section at RB. Percent Bad Ensembles averages 9%. StreamPro configuration was optimized for maximum velocity and depth of 1.5. This made a significant difference in the quality of data in the shallow section.  Change due to suggested Extrap automatic fit is >1% (-4.06% with Constant/No Slip 0.3092) and therefore applied. Sources:Import
Source:Manual
Source:Manual Sensor Reset Correction of 0.005
Source:Manual Sensor Reset Correction of 0.002

Measurement conducted on cableway with CableFox. Was necessary to use truck winch to hoist CableFox to platform level. Three CCs conducted. Third one used. Typical Heading Error: < 1.523°. Vector Addition Of Single And Double Cycle Errors: 1.826°. Two MBT loops conducted. Both fail with multiple errors. Three stationary MBTs conducted.  One passed. Multiple errors. GPS quality is good. Use GPS. GPS (GGA) reference used over VTG as HDOP remained below 2.0 for the majority of all transects. Extrap not used. Due to high flow, significant standing waves are present in center of channel. RB is poor with approximately 17m of sub-0.06m depth. Significant ambiguity velocities result from shallow depth. Sub-sectioning of the transects was done in post-processing to delete the shallow zones with ambiguity velocities. Shore pings were kept at the standard count of ten and the RB edge estimates were increased. River width accuracy is confirmed by historical cableway measurements at similar gauge heights. Multiple QA/QC issues arise with the application of sub-sectioning, notably: measured Q is reduced to 33.68%, RB edge is in excess of 5% of total Q and exposure time is significantly reduced. Sources:Import
Source:Manual Sensor Reset Correction of 0.002
